data.content:
Found it. The `GenerateSampleCode` target generates the `.cs` file but never adds it to the `Compile` item group, so the C# compiler doesn't know about it.

**Fix:** Add the generated file to `Compile` items after writing it.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target runs `BeforeTargets="CoreCompile;BeforeCompile"` and generates the file, but it never adds the generated file to the `Compile` item group. The MSBuild `Compile` items are collected before `CoreCompile`, so the generated file isn't included in compilation.

The fix is to add the generated file to the `Compile` item group within the target using `<ItemGroup>` with `<Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />`.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

    <ItemGroup>

    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.content:
Build succeeds and runs correctly now.

**Root cause:** The `GenerateSampleCode` target wrote `GeneratedInfo.cs` to disk but never added it to the `Compile` item group. The C# compiler only compiles files listed in `Compile`, so the generated file was ignored.

**Fix:** Added an `<ItemGroup><Compile Include="..." /></ItemGroup>` inside the target so the generated file is included in compilation.
